findings.title: Meta description is short
findings.recommendation: Aim for 140–160 characters to use the full SERP snippet.

findings.title: Heading levels skip a level
findings.detail: An H1 is followed by an H3.
findings.recommendation: Keep heading nesting sequential (no H2→H4 jumps).

findings.title: Canonical points elsewhere
findings.detail: Canonical is https://docs.python.org/3/index.html but page URL is https://docs.python.org/3/.

findings.title: Links with no anchor text
findings.recommendation: Give links descriptive anchor text for users and crawlers.

findings.title: Thin content
findings.recommendation: Expand to cover the topic fully; thin pages struggle to rank and are rarely cited by AI answers.

findings.title: Content is fairly hard to read
findings.recommendation: Consider shorter sentences/simpler words unless the audience is specialist.

findings.title: Dominant topics detected
findings.detail: python (7), documentation (4), theme (3), auto (3), light (3), dark (3)  ·  phrases: theme auto, auto light, light dark

findings.title: No visible author/byline
findings.detail: No author meta tag or byline detected near the top.
findings.recommendation: Add a named author with credentials — a core E-E-A-T (experience/expertise) signal.

findings.title: No Twitter/X card type
findings.recommendation: Add twitter:card (e.g. summary_large_image) for X previews.

findings.title: No structured data (JSON-LD)
findings.detail: The page has no application/ld+json markup.
findings.recommendation: Add JSON-LD for the page's entity (Article, Product, Organization…) to unlock rich results and clarify meaning for AI search.

findings.title: Few question-based headings
findings.detail: 0 of 8 subheadings are phrased as questions.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite key H2/H3s as the questions users actually ask — AI engines match answers to question-shaped headings.

findings.title: No statistics or citations
findings.detail: No numbers, data points, or source references detected.
findings.recommendation: Add concrete stats and cite primary sources — AI answers favor and attribute evidence-backed content.

findings.title: No llms.txt
findings.recommendation: Optionally publish /llms.txt to guide AI crawlers to your key content. Evidence for ranking impact is still thin — treat as low-cost, low-certainty.
